This ma'am was quite sweet and helpful

She explained and corrected me in things I was lacking

1.) whether you have eda, feature extraction, feature selection, preprocessors, splitting, models, hyperparameter tuning, confusion matrix, model comparision

2.) have you written like a list of insights from your EDA

3.) even if you have done one or two good feature extractions (good being the key word!), like making a new column by combing a few of them, or making an entirely new columns out of an existing one, she'll be impressed



Rest she was quite friendly so don't sweat too much!

column5:
- Explain the notebook
- EDA part
- Model explanation
- Explain the hyperparameters

These are what she asked -
1. Explain the notebook – Overall walkthrough of the code and process.

2. Feature engineering – Techniques and logic used.

3. Simple Imputer – Why mean, median, and mode are used.

4. Oversampling issue – What it is and when it causes problems.

5. Encoders – Target encoding vs One-Hot encoding; general use of encoders.

6. Model explanation – Detailed explanation of any one model used.

7. Model rejection – Why some models were discarded.

8. Graph interpretation – Graphs used and what insights they give.

9. Feature engineering in production – Its role and effect on latency.

10. Hyperparameters – What they are and how they were tuned.

11. Company practices – How companies clean datasets and use ML in production.

12. Personal questions – About you and your academic background.
column6: She was incredibly sweet and supportive. The key is to explain your problem statement clearly, walk through each cell with reasoning, and avoid using complex terms just to sound smart. Keep your answers simple, industry relevant, and stick to what's on the sheet.

I was asked to go over my notebook. I told them about my reasoning behind my data preprocessing and modeling steps. Explained to the proctor about 3 to 4 models that I have done. I was then asked about future scope of this project, things that can be done to improve the score better. I told him about some steps that I wanted to take after this viva to improve my model. To end with, I asked him if he had any suggestions regarding my pre-processing steps. 

Proctor was satisfied with my work and explanations.

This is a very good procter and one cannot pin-point the exact questions. Almost everyone who got this proctor had different set of questions during the viva. The reason is , he don't use any pre-made set of questions. He is at senior position in Ford Motors and have good knowledge , thus he will let you choose the topics where u can answer comfortably. 

For instance while you go through the models during notebook explanation , he will ask you , which is your strongest model out of them , and can you explain any model in detail. 

My sole suggestion is whatever you have written in the notebook should be on your fingertip with all the ins and out of the function / model / paramemters / methods / APIs used . Be honest with him , when you are unable to answer somehting.

Untill now he didn't ask any live coding modeling or pre processing question in colab with his dummy dataset. All he did was , notebook explanation and 6-7 questions .

Here are my questions :-

1. Can you explain Logistic Regression's role in SVM.

2. What is logistic regression ?

3. Among the models you used in your notebook , which model you can explain in complete detail ? (Choose honestly and wisely , he really asks in greater depth , for me it was Random forest or XGB )

(He persumed that i am good in using ensembles and started asking further questions)

4. How does Random Forest works ?

5. What is Bagging method and how it is different from Boosting ?

6. What are the loss functions in Random Forest ?

7. What are the regularization methods for Random Forest ?

8. Could you explain some optimization methods ?

9. If you have 10 DecisionTrees and 5 of them classified an input as 0 and 5 of them classified it as 1 , then what will be the final classification and how ?

10. Do you know any other evaluation metric apart from accuracy ? (I choosed AUC-ROC)

11. Explain me about AUC - ROC .

12. Explain each component of formula involved in AUC-ROC

He gave me a question to solve on pen and paper regarding AUC-ROC , here is the question :-

13. Suppose a data have 2 features F1 and F2 and 5 samples . The target variable of these samples in order are 1 , -1 , 1 , -1 , 1. On using RandomForest Classifier you got the prediction on the same training data to be 1 , 1 , 1 , -1 , 1 . How you will plot the AUC - ROC plot for this data ?

14. Suppose you have multi class insteaed of binary class , can you plot the AUC-ROC curve in a single plot ? If yes how ?
